description: Legal document in the hand of Ḥalfon b. Menashshe ha-Levi. Deathbed declaration of an old man, bringing his two sons before four witnesses and explaining that all he possesses in cash is 33 gold pieces. In view of such a small estate, he was afraid they would quarrel, wherefore he decided to arrange the distribution while still capable. The testator is [...] b. Moshe ha-Levi al-Ramlī. His sons are Abū ʿImrān Moshe and Abū l-Faraj. His wife may be named Dhukhr (l. 10). Dated November 1103 CE (Ḥeshvan 1416 Sel.). with crude signatures by Mevasser b. Ḥalfon, Yefet b. Yosef, Mevorakh b. ʿEli and Nissim b. Yaʿaqov (of which the first two and the second two look like they are each in the same hand). (Information from CUDL and Mediterranean Society, III, p. 409; V, pp. 133, 134, 542)
description_he: מסמך משפטי בכתב חלפון ב' מנשה. הצהרת גוסס.
